entries.title: Welcome to Sunflower
entries.body: Sunflower Early Learning is a family-owned preschool in South Austin, serving children from six weeks to five years old. We're a single-site center by design: small enough that every teacher knows every child by name, and warm enough that drop-off feels more like family than logistics. Our founder, Director Maya Okonkwo, opened Sunflower in 2019 after fifteen years teaching in public Pre-K, and leads the team today. We believe early learning should be playful, joyful, and rooted in real relationships — with teachers, with peers, and with the families who trust us with their kids.

entries.title: Main Office Contact
entries.body: Sunflower Early Learning is located at 1420 Willow Creek Ln, Austin, TX 78704. Main phone: (512) 555-0142. Email: hello@sunflower.care. Our office is staffed Monday through Friday from 7:00 am to 6:00 pm. The best time to reach us by phone is between 9:00 am and 11:00 am, when the rooms are quieter. For non-urgent questions you can also message us through the Sunflower family app — we check it throughout the day and reply as soon as we're able.

entries.title: Hours of Operation
entries.body: Sunflower is open Monday through Friday from 7:00 am to 6:00 pm. We ask families to aim for drop-off by 9:30 am so children can settle into the morning routine with their friends; late arrivals miss breakfast and the opening circle, which are some of the warmest parts of the day. We're closed on weekends and on the holidays listed in the yearly calendar you'll receive at enrollment.

entries.title: Holidays and Closure Calendar
entries.body: Sunflower closes for the following each year: New Year's Day, Martin Luther King Jr. Day, Memorial Day, Juneteenth, Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving (Thursday and Friday), and one week between Christmas and New Year's. We also close for four staff development days across the year — dates are shared in the family calendar at the start of each school year so you can plan ahead. Tuition is not prorated for holiday closures; these dates are built into your monthly rate.

entries.title: Ages We Welcome
entries.body: Sunflower welcomes children from six weeks through five years old, across four age groups: Infants (6 weeks to 12 months), Toddlers (12 to 24 months), Twos (2 to 3 years), and Preschool (3 to 5 years). Children transition to the next room when they're developmentally ready, not strictly on their birthday — our lead teachers and Director Maya meet with each family to time the move so it feels like a celebration, not a surprise.

entries.title: Classroom Ratios and Group Sizes
entries.body: We keep our ratios well below the Texas state minimums so every child gets real attention. Infants: 1 teacher for every 4 children, with a maximum group size of 8. Toddlers: 1 to 6, max group size 12. Twos: 1 to 8, max group size 16. Preschool: 1 to 10, max group size 20. Every classroom has at least two teachers on the floor throughout the day — we never leave a single teacher alone with a group.

entries.title: Tuition by Age Group
entries.body: Tuition at Sunflower is billed monthly and covers full-time, year-round enrollment. Infant: $1,680 per month. Toddler: $1,560 per month. Twos: $1,470 per month. Preschool: $1,380 per month. Families with more than one child enrolled receive a 10% sibling discount on the younger child's monthly tuition. Tuition includes meals, snacks, diapers for infants and toddlers, and all classroom materials — there are no extra supply fees during the year.

entries.title: When Tuition Is Due and How to Pay
entries.body: Tuition is due on the 1st of each month and is considered late after the 5th. We bill through the Sunflower family app, which supports ACH bank transfer (no fee) and credit card (3% processing fee added). If you prefer a physical check, you can drop it off at the front desk — please make it payable to Sunflower Early Learning LLC. A late payment fee of $35 is added after the 5th of the month. If you're ever in a tight spot, please reach out to Director Maya directly — we've worked out short-term payment plans with families before and would rather do that than have you worry alone.

entries.title: Late Pickup Fee
entries.body: Sunflower closes at 6:00 pm. If you arrive after 6:00 pm, a late pickup fee of $1 per minute is added to your next monthly bill. We understand emergencies happen — please call the main office at (512) 555-0142 as soon as you know you'll be late and we'll keep your child company. If a child is left at Sunflower 30 minutes after closing and we haven't been able to reach you or any emergency contact, we're required to call the Austin Police Department's non-emergency line and Texas DFPS.

entries.title: Registration and Annual Supply Fee
entries.body: There is a one-time registration fee of $150 per child when you enroll, and an annual supply fee of $120 per child charged each August. The registration fee holds your child's spot and covers the initial paperwork, health screening review, and welcome visit. The annual supply fee covers art materials, classroom books, and outdoor program equipment refresh for the year. Both fees are non-refundable after your child's first day.

entries.title: How to Enroll
entries.body: Enrolling at Sunflower is a four-step process. First, schedule a tour by calling (512) 555-0142 or emailing hello@sunflower.care — tours are offered Tuesday and Thursday mornings by appointment. Second, if we have a spot in your child's age group, we'll send an enrollment packet for you to complete online. Third, you'll meet with Director Maya for a 30-minute family intake to talk about your child's routines, any specific needs, and what you're hoping Sunflower can be for your family. Fourth, you'll pay the registration fee and schedule a phased start — most families start with a half day, then a full day, so the transition is gentle.

entries.title: What to Bring for Enrollment
entries.body: At enrollment we'll need: your child's birth certificate (copy is fine), current immunization records signed by their pediatrician OR a Texas Department of State Health Services affidavit of exemption, a completed emergency contact form listing at least two authorized pickup adults with ID, a signed parent handbook acknowledgement, and the first month's tuition plus the $150 registration fee. If your child has a diagnosed allergy or special health need, please also bring a doctor-signed care plan so we can set things up right before day one.

entries.title: Waitlist
entries.body: When a room is full, we start a waitlist in order of tour date. The waitlist is free to join and carries no commitment. Infant spots typically open up in August and January; toddler and older rooms have more flexibility throughout the year. Director Maya personally reaches out when a spot opens — we don't send automated wait-list emails, because this is the kind of decision families deserve a real conversation about.

entries.title: Scheduling a Tour
entries.body: We love showing families around Sunflower. Tours run Tuesday and Thursday mornings at 9:30 am and last about 45 minutes — you'll see each classroom during active program time, meet at least one lead teacher, and have a sit-down with Director Maya to ask anything on your mind. To schedule, call (512) 555-0142 or email hello@sunflower.care. Please bring your little one along if you'd like — we enjoy meeting them, and it's a great first taste of the space.

entries.title: What a Typical Day Looks Like
entries.body: A typical preschool day at Sunflower starts with open play and breakfast from 7:00 am to 8:30 am. Morning circle is at 8:45 am, followed by small-group program time from 9:00 to 10:30 am and outdoor play from 10:30 to 11:30 am. Lunch is served family-style at 11:30 am. Quiet rest is 12:30 to 2:30 pm (children who don't sleep rest quietly with books). Afternoon snack is at 2:45 pm, followed by more outdoor play, art or music, and open exploration until pickup. Infant and toddler days follow each child's own rhythm more than a fixed schedule — we sleep, eat, and play on demand in the younger rooms.

entries.title: Our Curriculum
entries.body: Sunflower uses a blend of Reggio Emilia-inspired project work and Creative Curriculum for Preschool. That means children's interests shape the week — if a classroom is fascinated by ants, we'll spend a week on ants, with books, art, dramatic play, and an outdoor magnifying-glass hunt. We track each child's development with ongoing observational notes and the Ages and Stages Questionnaire (ASQ-3), which we share with families at each parent-teacher conference. Our goal is not to get children ready for a standardized test — it's to send them to kindergarten curious, confident, and loved.

entries.title: Meals and Snacks
entries.body: Sunflower serves breakfast, lunch, and afternoon snack every day, prepared in-house by our kitchen team. Menus run on a 4-week rotation and are posted in the family app each Sunday for the upcoming week. All meals are nut-free (we're a peanut- and tree-nut-free building). We serve whole milk, water, and 100% fruit juice only — no flavored drinks. Infant formula and puréed foods are provided for babies on age-appropriate schedules; if your infant is breastfed, you can drop off labeled, dated bottles each morning and we'll store them in the infant-room fridge.

entries.title: Food Allergies
entries.body: If your child has a food allergy, we'll need a doctor-signed allergy action plan before their first day. The plan goes in their classroom binder and a copy sits on the fridge in our kitchen so every staff member who touches meals sees it. Children with allergies eat at a dedicated corner of the table with their own plate and utensils, and their teacher double-checks every dish before serving. Sunflower is peanut- and tree-nut-free — we do not make exceptions, even for packed lunches or birthday treats. For allergies beyond nuts, our kitchen works with families to adapt the menu where we can.

entries.title: Birthday Celebrations
entries.body: We love celebrating birthdays at Sunflower. Families are welcome to bring a store-bought, nut-free treat to share with the class — just let your child's teacher know a couple of days in advance so we can plan around allergies and nap schedules. We also offer a non-food option: bringing in a favorite book to donate to the classroom library, with your child's name written inside the cover. Many families do both.

entries.title: Immunization Requirements
entries.body: Texas requires that all children enrolled in licensed child care have an up-to-date immunization record on file. We ask for a copy signed by your pediatrician at enrollment, and an updated copy each time a new dose is due. Families who decline immunization for medical or conscientious reasons can provide a current Texas DSHS affidavit of exemption instead — we accept both. If your child is behind on any dose, we'll give you a gentle reminder about a month before state licensing requires the update.

entries.title: When to Keep Your Child Home
entries.body: Please keep your child home if they have any of the following: a fever of 100.4°F or higher (children must be fever-free for 24 hours without medication before returning), vomiting or diarrhea within the last 24 hours, an undiagnosed rash, pink eye or symptoms of pink eye, a persistent cough that interferes with play or sleep, or any contagious condition like strep, chicken pox, or hand-foot-and-mouth. If your child becomes sick during the day, we'll call you right away and ask you to pick them up within the hour. When in doubt, please call us — we'd rather talk it through than guess.

entries.title: Medication Policy
entries.body: Sunflower staff can administer medication only with a signed parent authorization form AND, for prescription medication, a doctor's note. Medication must be in its original labeled container with your child's name on it. We store all medication in a locked cabinet in the classroom, out of children's reach, and log every dose in the family app so you can see exactly when it was given. We do not administer cough drops (choking hazard) or homeopathic remedies without a pediatrician's written direction. For children with severe allergies, we keep their EpiPen in the classroom with their teacher, not in the locked cabinet, so it's reachable in seconds.

entries.title: Sunscreen
entries.body: Sunflower staff apply sunscreen to every child before outdoor time whenever the UV index is 3 or higher. We ask each family to bring in a single labeled bottle of their child's preferred sunscreen — SPF 30 or higher is ideal — and we'll store it in the classroom cubby. Please sign the sunscreen permission form at enrollment; we can't apply it without one. We also seek shade whenever possible and encourage sun hats during outdoor play.

entries.title: Arrival and Departure
entries.body: Every child must be walked into their classroom by an authorized adult — no drop-offs from the parking lot. Please sign your child in on the Sunflower family app as you arrive, and sign them out at pickup. Our parking lot is small; please be mindful of other families coming and going, and never leave a sibling alone in the car. If your child is old enough to notice, a short, warm goodbye tends to go better than a long, drawn-out one — our teachers are happy to help with the hand-off.

entries.title: Authorized Pickup List
entries.body: Only adults on your child's authorized pickup list can take them home from Sunflower. Please list at least two authorized adults in addition to the enrolling parents at enrollment, and update the list anytime it changes — grandparents visiting, new sitter, custody change, anything. Every authorized adult must be 18 or older and will be asked to show photo ID the first time they pick up, even if we've met them before. If someone not on the list arrives, we will not release the child — we'll call you first, without exception.

entries.title: Custody and Separated Families
entries.body: Sunflower remains neutral in family custody matters. If there is a court-ordered parenting plan in place, please bring a copy at enrollment and we'll keep it in your child's file. Unless a court order says otherwise, both legal parents have equal rights to pick up their child. If custody changes during your time with us, please let Director Maya know right away and bring updated documentation. We will not share custody-related information between parents — that's yours to share or not.

entries.title: Emergency Procedures
entries.body: Sunflower teachers are trained in pediatric first aid, CPR, and emergency evacuation. In a medical emergency, we follow this order: (1) administer first aid as trained, (2) call 911 if needed, (3) call you, (4) call your child's pediatrician if appropriate. For fires or other building emergencies, we evacuate to the rally point at the Willow Creek Park playground across the street and call families from there. We run quarterly fire drills and an annual severe-weather drill with the kids so emergency procedures feel familiar, not scary.

entries.title: Weather Closures
entries.body: When severe weather makes it unsafe to travel, Sunflower closes for the day. We follow Austin Independent School District's weather-closure announcements — if AISD closes, we close. We announce closures by 6:00 am via the family app push notification and an email to the address on your file. Closures for weather do not result in a tuition refund; we schedule make-up days into the yearly calendar when we can.

entries.title: Rest and Nap Time
entries.body: Quiet rest is part of every day at Sunflower. Preschool rest time is from 12:30 pm to 2:30 pm — children who sleep, sleep, and children who don't rest quietly on their mat with books. Toddlers and Twos typically nap from about 12:30 to 2:30 as well, adjusted for each child's rhythm. Infants nap on their own schedules throughout the day. Families are welcome to bring a small blanket or stuffed lovey from home; we'll send it home each Friday for laundering.

entries.title: Guidance and Discipline
entries.body: At Sunflower we think of discipline as guidance — helping children understand their feelings, their bodies, and how to be in community with each other. We use positive redirection, clear and consistent limits, and calm problem-solving. We do not use time-outs in isolation, shame, food or bathroom withdrawal, physical punishment, or raised voices as discipline. When a child is having a hard moment, a teacher sits with them, names the feeling, and helps them reset. When bigger patterns emerge, we loop in the family early — we'd rather have a conversation at week two than a crisis at month two.

entries.title: Children with Special Needs and IEPs
entries.body: Sunflower is inclusive by default. We welcome children with IEPs, IFSPs, diagnosed disabilities, and developmental differences, and we partner with outside specialists (speech, OT, PT, behavioral) who come into the classroom to support children in their regular routine. If your child has an existing IEP or IFSP, please bring a copy at enrollment and we'll build their classroom plan around it. If a need emerges during their time with us, we'll talk with you about next steps — usually starting with a referral to Early Childhood Intervention (ECI) for the under-3 set or the local public school district's evaluation team for older children.

entries.title: Parent-Teacher Conferences
entries.body: We hold formal parent-teacher conferences twice a year — once in the fall and once in the spring — with your child's lead teacher and Director Maya if you'd like her there. Conferences are 30 minutes, scheduled through the family app, and cover your child's development, friendships, and anything you're curious or worried about. Outside of the scheduled conferences, you can request a meeting any time by messaging your child's teacher — we'd rather talk something through sooner than later.

entries.title: How Teachers Stay in Touch
entries.body: Every classroom at Sunflower uses the family app to share daily highlights, photos, nap and meal logs, and any notes about your child's day. Infants and toddlers get more frequent updates (bottles, diapers, naps) since those details matter at that age. Older children get a short daily recap plus photos. Teachers answer family-app messages during rest time and after 5:30 pm pickup, so please don't worry if you don't hear back immediately — they're on the floor with the kids.

entries.title: Open-Door Policy
entries.body: Families are welcome to visit Sunflower any time during open hours — no appointment needed. If you want to drop in for lunch with your child, join a story time, or just sit in the reading nook for a few minutes at pickup, we'd love to have you. The only ask is that you sign in at the front desk so we know who's in the building. We find that open doors build more trust than any policy statement ever could.

entries.title: Concerns and Feedback
entries.body: If something about your child's day or our program is bothering you, please tell us. Start with your child's lead teacher — most things can be sorted out in a five-minute conversation at pickup. If it's bigger than that or you'd rather not start with the teacher, reach out to Director Maya directly at (512) 555-0142 or hello@sunflower.care. We take feedback seriously and we don't hold it against families. The fastest way for us to get better is to hear from you when something doesn't feel right.

entries.title: Withdrawing from Sunflower
entries.body: If your family needs to move on from Sunflower, we ask for two weeks' written notice so we can plan the transition for your child's classmates and offer the spot to a waitlisted family. There is no withdrawal fee. Your final month's tuition is prorated based on your last day. We'll send a summary of your child's time at Sunflower — favorite activities, friendships, growth we noticed — so you have a keepsake of their time here.

entries.title: Our Team
entries.body: Sunflower is led by Director Maya Okonkwo, who founded the center in 2019 after fifteen years teaching public Pre-K. Every lead teacher holds at least a CDA (Child Development Associate) credential or an associate's degree in early childhood education, and several have bachelor's degrees. All teaching staff complete annual pediatric first aid, CPR, and trauma-informed care training. We intentionally hire and retain a small, stable team — most of our teachers have been with us more than two years, which is unusual in this field and something we work hard to maintain.
